response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a highly experienced senior/staff-level engineer with 15+ years of professional experience, making him significantly overqualified for an entry-level position. While he has Java in his skill set and solid agile methodology exposure, two critical disqualifiers stand out: his German is self-described as only 'Basic,' which conflicts directly with the role's requirement for strong verbal and written German communication with clients in a consulting context; and his background shows zero exposure to the insurance sector. The seniority mismatch would also create retention and compensation alignment challenges.
strengths: Strong computer science foundation with a BS from MIT and 15+ years of hands-on software development experience
Proven agile practitioner (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) with documented cloud experience (AWS CDK)
Java is explicitly listed as a skill with direct usage at Altom Consulting for robotic arm projects
concerns: Severely overqualified — Staff/Senior-level experience is misaligned with an entry-level role, creating obvious retention risk
German proficiency listed as 'Basic,' which directly conflicts with the requirement for strong client-facing German communication in a consulting environment
No exposure to the insurance sector or insurance-domain digitalization projects whatsoever
